# PR #7 — Standalone iroh + Automerge Android P2P experiment

source: https://github.com/croftc/SecurityPolicy/pull/7

captured: 2026-06-15 (via cpettet_croftc). State at capture: OPEN. 23 files, +8057/-0.

branch: claude/delta-chat-android-iroh-16o28h → main

> Code-forward experiment: a Delta-Chat-inspired Android app over a Rust core (UniFFI
> bindings) doing two-peer Automerge document sync over REAL iroh. Tier 1 verified
> (cargo test green incl. two-peer sync); APK assembly is toolchain-gated, not code-gated
> (see PATH_TO_APK.md). Companion transcript: CODING-TRANSCRIPT.md. jniLibs .so not
> committed (built locally); SecurityPolicy plumbing not imported.

---

## PR description

## What this is

A **self-contained** experiment in `experiments/android-p2p-app/` that wires a Rust P2P stack — **iroh** (transport) + **Automerge** (CRDT) — into a *truly basic* private-group read/post app, bridged to an Android Kotlin UI through a single JSON-in / JSON-out FFI call. It is structured along Delta Chat's proven, shipping iroh-in-Android patterns.

> Lives under `experiments/`, well clear of `Publish/`, so the Confluence sync is untouched.

## Delta Chat patterns emulated
- **JSON-RPC-over-FFI binding**: one `GroupClient.handle(json) -> json` method instead of per-method C-FFI/JNI (UniFFI generates the typed Kotlin + JNA loading).
- **iroh started lazily** (only on create/join group), as an ephemeral channel.
- **Bootstrap by NodeAddr + 32-byte TopicId**, with the **direct IP deliberately excluded** from the invite.

## The load-bearing proof (green here)
`core/tests/sync_over_iroh.rs` binds **two real iroh endpoints**, dials over loopback (hermetic — relay/DNS disabled), and exchanges Automerge snapshots over a real QUIC bidirectional stream in **both directions**. Post-on-A → sync → read-on-B and vice versa; both documents converge. All 5 host tests pass.

## Capability tier: **Tier 1** (+ Kotlin bindings generated)
This environment has **no Android NDK and no SDK**, so it cannot cross-compile a `.so` or assemble an APK — as the brief predicted. Delivered: a complete, correct, buildable project; the Rust core compiles; all host tests pass; Kotlin bindings generated; a complete Android/Gradle project consumes them; build instructions for Tiers 2/3 documented.

Pinned versions: `iroh 0.98.2`, `automerge 0.6.1`, `uniffi 0.29.5`, `tokio 1.52.3`.

## Honest friction log (in README)
- `iroh 0.98` + `automerge 0.10` **can't coexist** (`sha2` RC-vs-stable conflict) → pinned `automerge 0.6.1`.
- `iroh-gossip` dropped (transitive `ed25519-dalek`/`sha2` conflicts) → used direct iroh `Endpoint` connections over a custom ALPN; gossip is the documented scale-up.
- Async-across-FFI handled with blocking calls on `Dispatchers.IO` (Delta Chat's guidance), no Rust→Kotlin callbacks.

## Deferred (honestly)
On-device/multi-emulator run + real APK (needs SDK/NDK); live relay-path sync across NATs; encryption (noted where it'd wrap payloads); background/push/persistence/multi-group.

See [`experiments/android-p2p-app/README.md`](https://github.com/croftc/SecurityPolicy/blob/claude/delta-chat-android-iroh-16o28h/experiments/android-p2p-app/README.md) for the full report.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01GzQeiVwqJXNjUQGFNv2B2m

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Android app for private P2P group chat: create/join groups, generate/share invites, post and view messages, and sync across peers. App requests network permissions and includes a minimal launcher UI.

* **Documentation**
  * Full README, PATH_TO_APK guide, and jniLibs notes with architecture, build/run steps, and troubleshooting.

* **Tests**
  * Integration tests validating end-to-end message synchronization between peers.

* **Chores**
  * Android build/tooling added: Gradle config, wrappers, plugin bumps, gradle.properties, and gitignore for build artifacts.

## Walkthrough

This PR adds experiments/android-p2p-app: an Android app and Rust core (iroh + Automerge) bridged by a JSON-in/JSON-out UniFFI FFI surface, generated Kotlin/JNA bindings, Android build/wrappers, a minimal UI, and an integration test that verifies snapshot sync between two peers.

## Changes

**Android P2P Experiment Implementation**

|Layer / File(s)|Summary|
|---|---|
|**Android Build & Configuration** <br> `experiments/android-p2p-app/.gitignore`, `android/app/build.gradle.kts`, `android/build.gradle.kts`, `android/gradle.properties`, `android/gradle/wrapper/*`, `android/gradlew*`, `android/settings.gradle.kts`, `android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml`, `android/app/src/main/jniLibs/README.md`|Gradle root/app configs pinned to current plugin versions, Gradle 8.9 wrapper scripts, NDK ABI filters (arm64-v8a, x86_64), JNA + AndroidX dependencies, manifest declares network permissions and `.MainActivity` launcher, and `.gitignore` for build outputs.|
|**Protocol & Command Surface** <br> `core/Cargo.toml`, `core/src/protocol.rs`|Cargo.toml defines `group-core` crate (rlib+cdylib) and pins dependencies; `Command`/`Response` enums define the JSON boundary and `Invite` encodes node-id + optional relay-url (no direct IP) as `croftcgrp1:` JSON+base64url with size guard and tests.|
|**Automerge Group Document** <br> `core/src/group.rs`|`Message` struct and `GroupDoc` wrapper around Automerge AutoCommit, with `new_group` (genesis), `new_empty` (joiner), `post`, `messages`, `snapshot`, and `merge_snapshot`; unit tests verify roundtrip and convergence.|
|**iroh P2P Transport** <br> `core/src/net.rs`|`P2pNode` wraps iroh Endpoint, supports relay-enabled (N0) or relay-disabled loopback (Minimal), and provides `connect_and_exchange` / `accept_and_exchange` for bounded snapshot exchange over a bidirectional QUIC stream with timeouts.|
|**Session Management & Command Orchestration** <br> `core/src/lib.rs`, `core/src/bin/uniffi-bindgen.rs`|`Session` holds identity, runtime, GroupDoc, and lazily-started P2pNode; `handle(cmd)` implements Init/Create/Join/Post/GetMessages/Sync flows with snapshot exchange at join; `GroupClient` UniFFI object wraps Session and exposes `handle(String) -> String`.|
|**UniFFI Kotlin Bindings (Generated)** <br> `android/app/src/main/java/uniffi/group_core/group_core.kt`|Generated Kotlin/JNA layer (1.4k+ lines): RustBuffer types, `FfiConverter` trait and implementations (String, GroupClient), Rust-call status/error lifting, JNA library loading with API/version & checksum integrity checks, `UniffiCleaner`, and concurrency-safe GroupClient wrapper with in-flight tracking.|
|**Android UI & Group Client Integration** <br> `android/app/src/main/java/com/croftc/p2pexp/MainActivity.kt`|AppCompatActivity with message list, input, and buttons for post/get-invite/join; `onCreate()` initializes GroupClient and create-group; `send()` uses `Dispatchers.IO`; `refresh()` renders `author: text` lines.|
|**Integration Test & Documentation** <br> `core/tests/sync_over_iroh.rs`, `experiments/android-p2p-app/README.md`, `PATH_TO_APK.md`|Integration test runs two real P2pNode peers on loopback, exchanges snapshots in two phases, merges into GroupDoc instances, and asserts convergence; README and PATH_TO_APK document scope, build tiers (NDK/SDK needed for on-device), binding surface, invite format, friction log, and proven vs deferred checklist.|

## Suggested reviewers

- croftc/infosec

Be direct: one actionable item — Invite encoding strips direct IP transports and reconstructs EndpointAddr from node_id and optional relay_url. Risk parameters: invite string content and relay_url parsing (input: external invite text; internal: relay_url field). Fix: validate and document relay discovery assumptions and enforce that any relay_url used is a vetted relay endpoint (e.g., compare against an allowlist or require explicit operator configuration) and add unit tests ensuring Invite::decode rejects malformed relay_url forms and that endpoint reconstruction cannot produce non-empty IP transport addrs.

### cpettet_croftc — 2026-06-13T14:18:33Z

Addressed the CodeRabbit review in 506e632. Applied 6 fixes (1 correctness + 5 hardening):

- **Reset `peer` on `CreateGroup`** — prevents a best-effort post-sync from merging a fresh group's doc into a previously-joined group's stale peer.
- **No panic at the FFI boundary** — a poisoned session mutex now returns a JSON error instead of crashing the caller.
- **Network timeouts** — a 15s deadline on each iroh wait (`connect`/`open_bi`/`accept`/`accept_bi`/stream read+write/`closed`) and on the relay `online()` wait, so an unresponsive peer can't hang the synchronous command path. The listen-for-incoming wait stays open-ended by design.
- **Bounded invite size** — `Invite::decode` rejects oversized bodies before base64/JSON decode.
- **Android:** `init` payload built via `JSONObject` (escapes `Build.MODEL`); `GroupClient.close()` in `onDestroy` to release native session state.

All host tests still pass, including the two-peer Automerge sync over real iroh.

Intentionally **not** changed: comments on `gradlew` and the UniFFI `RustBuffer` layout (both generated files, not hand-written), and the note that `accept_and_exchange` isn't wired into a live session accept-loop — that's the documented deferred live-sync path, not a bug.

````
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In
`@experiments/android-p2p-app/android/app/src/main/java/com/croftc/p2pexp/MainActivity.kt`:
- Line 29: The Activity creates a GroupClient via "private val client:
GroupClient by lazy { GroupClient() }" but never calls close(), leaking
native/session resources; change the client to a nullable var (e.g., private var
client: GroupClient? = null), initialize it in onCreate (client =
GroupClient()), and call client?.close() and set client = null in onDestroy to
ensure cleanup; update call sites that use client.commandJson(...) to use the
safe-call (client?.commandJson(...)) or require-initialized client after
onCreate to avoid accidentally re-instantiating during teardown.
- Line 41: The send call currently interpolates android.os.Build.MODEL directly
into a raw JSON string; instead construct the payload with org.json.JSONObject
(e.g. create a JSONObject, put("cmd","init") and
put("author","android-"+android.os.Build.MODEL)), then call
send(jsonObject.toString()) so the author value is properly escaped; update the
send("""{"cmd":"init","author":"android-${'$'}{android.os.Build.MODEL}"}""")
invocation to build and send the JSONObject string.

In
`@experiments/android-p2p-app/android/app/src/main/java/uniffi/group_core/group_core.kt`:
- Around line 97-107: The RustBufferByReference struct is sized incorrectly and
reads the data pointer wrongly for 64-bit ABIs; update the class constructor to
allocate capacity + len + pointer bytes (use ByReference(16 +
Native.POINTER_SIZE) or ByReference(16 + Pointer.SIZE)) and change the
getValue() implementation to read the data field with pointer.getPointer(16)
(instead of pointer.getLong(16)); keep setValue as-is (it already uses
pointer.setPointer(16)). Ensure you import Native or Pointer.SIZE if needed and
reference the RustBufferByReference class and its getValue/setValue methods when
making these edits.

In `@experiments/android-p2p-app/android/gradlew`:
- Line 117: The CLASSPATH variable in the gradlew script is incorrectly set to
an empty escaped string (CLASSPATH="\\\"\\\"") which then gets passed to Java
via -classpath "$CLASSPATH" and can break wrapper startup; change the CLASSPATH
assignment to point to the wrapper JAR (e.g., set
CLASSPATH="$APP_HOME/gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar") so that when gradlew
invokes java with -classpath "$CLASSPATH" it includes the gradle-wrapper.jar
(leave the existing -jar "$APP_HOME/gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar" usage
unchanged).

In `@experiments/android-p2p-app/core/src/lib.rs`:
- Around line 100-105: When handling Command::CreateGroup (the match arm that
calls self.ensure_node(), sets self.topic = Some(...), and sets self.doc =
GroupDoc::new_group()), also reset the session peer by setting self.peer = None
to avoid carrying a stale peer into the new group; apply the same change to the
other CreateGroup handling sites in this file (the other match arms/blocks that
perform topic/doc initialization) so PostMessage best-effort sync cannot merge
with a stale remote group state.
- Around line 193-195: The handler currently calls
self.inner.lock().expect("session mutex poisoned") in handle, which can panic
across the FFI boundary; change that to handle a poisoned mutex gracefully by
matching the Result from self.inner.lock() (or using .lock().map_err(...)) and
returning a JSON error string instead of panicking. Concretely, in the handle
method, replace the expect call with a match or if let Err(e) branch that
constructs and returns a JSON error response (e.g.,
serde_json::to_string(&error_struct) or a manual JSON string) describing the
mutex error, and only proceed to parse Command and process the session when the
lock succeeds.
- Around line 100-141: The CreateGroup arm should clear any stale peer and start
an inbound accept/exchange loop on the created node, JoinGroup should keep
dialing (connect_and_exchange) but the host must be accepting incoming
accept_and_exchange calls to perform the initial snapshot transfer, and the
session mutex unwrap must be converted to an error return instead of panicking:
specifically, in the Command::CreateGroup arm (symbol: CreateGroup) set
self.peer = None after creating topic/doc and spawn or start the node accept
loop (call or delegate to accept_and_exchange/endpoint.accept() or start the
accept loop that performs the exchange) so the creator will handle incoming
JoinGroup exchanges; leave Command::JoinGroup using Invite::decode +
node.connect_and_exchange as-is (symbol: connect_and_exchange) but verify the
host accept implementation pairs with it; and in GroupClient::handle replace
self.inner.lock().expect("session mutex poisoned") with proper error handling
(map_err or match the PoisonError into an Err(Response::Error(...)) or propagate
an Error) to avoid panics. Ensure sync_with_peer (symbol: sync_with_peer) relies
on these changes and needs no further modification if host accept loop is
started.

In `@experiments/android-p2p-app/core/src/net.rs`:
- Around line 88-90: The async network methods (online, connect, accept and any
stream read/write calls) currently await indefinitely; wrap each awaited network
operation with a tokio::time::timeout and return a Result so callers can handle
timeouts instead of hanging: e.g., change the signature of online/connect/accept
to return Result<_, Error>, use tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(N),
self.endpoint.online()) (or similar per call) and map timeout::error() into a
descriptive timeout error, apply the same timeout wrapper around
stream.read/send futures, and propagate the Result up (adjust callers to handle
the error) so slow/non‑responsive peers do not block forever.

In `@experiments/android-p2p-app/core/src/protocol.rs`:
- Around line 91-99: Invite::decode accepts unbounded base64 input which can
cause large allocations; add size checks to bound both the incoming base64
string and the decoded JSON bytes before parsing: after obtaining body via
strip_prefix(INVITE_PREFIX), reject if body.len() exceeds a new constant
MAX_INVITE_B64_LEN (e.g. 4KB–16KB as appropriate), then after
BASE64URL_NOPAD.decode(...) check that json.len() <= MAX_INVITE_JSON_LEN (or
reuse a single MAX_INVITE_BYTES) and return an error if exceeded; keep these
checks in the same function (Invite::decode) and use clear error contexts (e.g.,
"invite too large (base64)" / "invite too large (decoded)") before calling
serde_json::from_slice.

---

Nitpick comments:
In `@experiments/android-p2p-app/README.md`:
- Around line 62-64: The fenced code block for the method signature
GroupClient.handle(commandJson: String): String should specify the language for
syntax highlighting; update the opening fence from ``` to ```kotlin so the block
reads as a Kotlin fenced code block (no other code changes needed).
- Around line 119-132: Change the fenced code block that shows the test output
so it includes a language specifier (use "text" or "console") on the opening
backticks; specifically replace the opening "```" before the test output with
"```text" so the test output is rendered correctly.
- Around line 144-153: Add a language specifier (e.g., text) to the fenced code
block that begins with "Kotlin UI (MainActivity)                      
android/app" so the diagram is rendered as plain text; specifically, change the
opening triple backticks to include the specifier (for example, ```text) in the
README's architecture diagram block.
- Around line 101-105: Add a language specifier to the fenced code block that
contains the invite structure lines (the block starting with "node_id    =
inviter's public key (iroh EndpointId)" and including "relay_url" and "topic");
update the opening fence from ``` to something like ```text or ```yaml so the
block is properly annotated (no other content changes required).
- Around line 50-54: The fenced code block containing the version listing (the
triple-backtick block that starts with the lines "iroh          0.98.2       
automerge   0.6.1        tokio    1.52.3" and ends after "ed25519-dalek
3.0.0-pre.6") needs a language specifier to satisfy markdown linting; change the
opening fence from ``` to ```text (or ```properties) so it becomes ```text and
leave the block contents unchanged.
````

</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In
`@experiments/android-p2p-app/android/app/src/main/java/com/croftc/p2pexp/MainActivity.kt`:
- Around line 48-53: The onDestroy implementation calls client.close() directly
which can throw and prevent super.onDestroy() from running; wrap the
client.close() call in a try/catch (catch Throwable) inside the onDestroy method
(referencing onDestroy, client.close(), and super.onDestroy()) so any exception
from client.close() is caught and logged but not rethrown, and ensure
super.onDestroy() is always invoked in a finally or after the catch block.
```

</details>

### coderabbitai[bot] on `experiments/android-p2p-app/android/app/src/main/java/com/croftc/p2pexp/MainActivity.kt` :L29 — 2026-06-13T13:51:51Z

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Close `GroupClient` during Activity teardown to avoid native/session leaks.**

`GroupClient` owns native state, but this Activity never calls `close()`/`destroy()`. On configuration changes or Activity recreation, previous sessions can remain alive and keep resources allocated while new `commandJson` calls use a new client.






<details>
<summary>🔧 Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
 class MainActivity : AppCompatActivity() {
@@
     override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
         super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
         setContentView(buildUi())
@@
         }
     }
+
+    override fun onDestroy() {
+        runCatching { client.close() }
+        super.onDestroy()
+    }
```
</details>


Also applies to: 35-45, 113-116

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In
`@experiments/android-p2p-app/android/app/src/main/java/com/croftc/p2pexp/MainActivity.kt`
at line 29, The Activity creates a GroupClient via "private val client:
GroupClient by lazy { GroupClient() }" but never calls close(), leaking
native/session resources; change the client to a nullable var (e.g., private var
client: GroupClient? = null), initialize it in onCreate (client =
GroupClient()), and call client?.close() and set client = null in onDestroy to
ensure cleanup; update call sites that use client.commandJson(...) to use the
safe-call (client?.commandJson(...)) or require-initialized client after
onCreate to avoid accidentally re-instantiating during teardown.
```

</details>

### coderabbitai[bot] on `experiments/android-p2p-app/android/app/src/main/java/com/croftc/p2pexp/MainActivity.kt` — 2026-06-13T13:51:51Z

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Build the `init` payload with `JSONObject` so `author` is escaped safely.**

Line 41 interpolates `android.os.Build.MODEL` directly into JSON. If that input contains quotes/backslashes/control chars, `commandJson` becomes invalid and `init` fails.






<details>
<summary>🔧 Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
-            send("""{"cmd":"init","author":"android-${'$'}{android.os.Build.MODEL}"}""")
+            send(
+                JSONObject()
+                    .put("cmd", "init")
+                    .put("author", "android-${android.os.Build.MODEL}")
+                    .toString()
+            )
```
</details>

```suggestion

                JSONObject()
                    .put("cmd", "init")
                    .put("author", "android-${android.os.Build.MODEL}")
                    .toString()
            )
```

`@experiments/android-p2p-app/android/app/src/main/java/com/croftc/p2pexp/MainActivity.kt`
at line 41, The send call currently interpolates android.os.Build.MODEL directly
into a raw JSON string; instead construct the payload with org.json.JSONObject
(e.g. create a JSONObject, put("cmd","init") and
put("author","android-"+android.os.Build.MODEL)), then call
send(jsonObject.toString()) so the author value is properly escaped; update the

---



</details>

**Fix `RustBufferByReference` struct sizing and `data` pointer read for 64-bit ABIs.**
- `RustBufferByReference : ByReference(16)` is too small for `RustBuffer`’s `capacity` (8) + `len` (8) + `data` pointer (8 on `arm64-v8a`/`x86_64`), so writes at offset `16` can go out of bounds.
- `getValue()` reads the `data` pointer with `pointer.getLong(16)` but the field is a `Pointer?`; it should use `pointer.getPointer(16)`.
- `RustBufferByReference` has no usages anywhere in the repo right now (only the class declaration), so this is likely dead/unexercised today—but it will break if UniFFI ever wires an out-pointer callback through it.

<details>
<summary>🐛 Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
-class RustBufferByReference : ByReference(16) {
+class RustBufferByReference : ByReference(16 + Native.POINTER_SIZE) {
@@
     fun getValue(): RustBuffer.ByValue {
         val pointer = getPointer()
         val value = RustBuffer.ByValue()
         value.writeField("capacity", pointer.getLong(0))
         value.writeField("len", pointer.getLong(8))
-        value.writeField("data", pointer.getLong(16))
+        value.writeField("data", pointer.getPointer(16))
 
         return value
     }
 }
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In
`@experiments/android-p2p-app/android/app/src/main/java/uniffi/group_core/group_core.kt`
around lines 97 - 107, The RustBufferByReference struct is sized incorrectly and
reads the data pointer wrongly for 64-bit ABIs; update the class constructor to
allocate capacity + len + pointer bytes (use ByReference(16 +
Native.POINTER_SIZE) or ByReference(16 + Pointer.SIZE)) and change the
getValue() implementation to read the data field with pointer.getPointer(16)
(instead of pointer.getLong(16)); keep setValue as-is (it already uses
pointer.setPointer(16)). Ensure you import Native or Pointer.SIZE if needed and
reference the RustBufferByReference class and its getValue/setValue methods when
making these edits.
```

</details>

**Fix `gradlew` wrapper classpath value (`CLASSPATH="\\\"\\\""`).**

In `experiments/android-p2p-app/android/gradlew`, `CLASSPATH` is set to `\\\"\\\"` (evaluates to `\"\"`) but then passed via `-classpath "$CLASSPATH"`. The script also correctly uses `-jar "$APP_HOME/gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar"` (so switching to `org.gradle.wrapper.GradleWrapperMain` is not the needed change for this template); the actionable bug is the bogus `CLASSPATH` value. On JVMs/environments where `-classpath` is honored, this can break wrapper startup; restore `CLASSPATH` to point at the wrapper jar (e.g., `$APP_HOME/gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar`).

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@experiments/android-p2p-app/android/gradlew` at line 117, The CLASSPATH
variable in the gradlew script is incorrectly set to an empty escaped string
(CLASSPATH="\\\"\\\"") which then gets passed to Java via -classpath
"$CLASSPATH" and can break wrapper startup; change the CLASSPATH assignment to
point to the wrapper JAR (e.g., set
CLASSPATH="$APP_HOME/gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar") so that when gradlew
invokes java with -classpath "$CLASSPATH" it includes the gradle-wrapper.jar
(leave the existing -jar "$APP_HOME/gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar" usage
unchanged).
```

</details>

### coderabbitai[bot] on `experiments/android-p2p-app/core/src/lib.rs` :L116 — 2026-06-13T13:51:51Z

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Reset `peer` when creating a new group to prevent cross-group merges.**

If this session previously joined another group, `CreateGroup` keeps the old `peer`. Then `PostMessage` best-effort sync can merge the new group doc with the stale remote group state.

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
 Command::CreateGroup => {
     self.ensure_node()?;
     self.topic = Some(rand::random::<[u8; 32]>());
     self.doc = GroupDoc::new_group()?;
+    self.peer = None;
     Ok(Response::Ok)
 }
```
</details>

   


Also applies to: 126-133, 146-151

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@experiments/android-p2p-app/core/src/lib.rs` around lines 100 - 105, When
handling Command::CreateGroup (the match arm that calls self.ensure_node(), sets
self.topic = Some(...), and sets self.doc = GroupDoc::new_group()), also reset
the session peer by setting self.peer = None to avoid carrying a stale peer into
the new group; apply the same change to the other CreateGroup handling sites in
this file (the other match arms/blocks that perform topic/doc initialization) so
PostMessage best-effort sync cannot merge with a stale remote group state.
```

</details>

**Fix missing inbound accept path + stale `peer` + remove panicking mutex unwrap.**

`JoinGroup` only dials (`connect_and_exchange`), and there is no call site anywhere in `experiments/android-p2p-app/core/src` that invokes `accept_and_exchange`—only its definition exists in `net.rs`. That leaves the creator/host side without an inbound accept/exchange loop for the initial snapshot transfer, so join/sync can fail or hang.

Also, `Command::CreateGroup` sets `topic` and initializes a new `GroupDoc`, but it does not reset `self.peer`, so a stale peer from a previous session can cause incorrect cross-group syncing.

Finally, `GroupClient::handle` does `self.inner.lock().expect("session mutex poisoned")`; avoid panicking on a poisoned mutex—convert it to an error return so a single poison can’t crash the process.

[critical actionable fix] Ensure the host/creator path runs an inbound `endpoint.accept()` + exchange (i.e., a real `accept_and_exchange` call or equivalent accept loop) during join/sync, clear `self.peer` when handling `CreateGroup`, and replace the mutex `expect(...)` with error handling.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@experiments/android-p2p-app/core/src/lib.rs` around lines 100 - 141, The
CreateGroup arm should clear any stale peer and start an inbound accept/exchange
loop on the created node, JoinGroup should keep dialing (connect_and_exchange)
but the host must be accepting incoming accept_and_exchange calls to perform the
initial snapshot transfer, and the session mutex unwrap must be converted to an
error return instead of panicking: specifically, in the Command::CreateGroup arm
(symbol: CreateGroup) set self.peer = None after creating topic/doc and spawn or
start the node accept loop (call or delegate to
accept_and_exchange/endpoint.accept() or start the accept loop that performs the

### coderabbitai[bot] on `experiments/android-p2p-app/core/src/lib.rs` :L212 — 2026-06-13T13:51:51Z

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Avoid panic on poisoned mutex at the FFI boundary.**

`expect("session mutex poisoned")` can panic and crash the caller path. Return a JSON error response instead of panicking.

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
 pub fn handle(&self, command_json: String) -> String {
-    let mut session = self.inner.lock().expect("session mutex poisoned");
+    let mut session = match self.inner.lock() {
+        Ok(guard) => guard,
+        Err(e) => {
+            return format!(
+                "{{\"status\":\"error\",\"message\":\"session lock poisoned: {}\"}}",
+                e
+            );
+        }
+    };
```
</details>

handler currently calls self.inner.lock().expect("session mutex poisoned") in
handle, which can panic across the FFI boundary; change that to handle a
poisoned mutex gracefully by matching the Result from self.inner.lock() (or
using .lock().map_err(...)) and returning a JSON error string instead of
panicking. Concretely, in the handle method, replace the expect call with a
match or if let Err(e) branch that constructs and returns a JSON error response

### coderabbitai[bot] on `experiments/android-p2p-app/core/src/net.rs` :L103 — 2026-06-13T13:51:52Z

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Add timeouts around network waits to avoid indefinite hangs.**

These paths await network operations without deadlines (`online`, `connect`, `accept`, stream reads/writes). A slow or non-responsive peer can block the command path indefinitely.

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix pattern</summary>

```diff
+use std::time::Duration;
+use tokio::time::timeout;
+
+const IO_TIMEOUT: Duration = Duration::from_secs(15);

- let conn = self.endpoint.connect(peer, ALPN).await?;
+ let conn = timeout(IO_TIMEOUT, self.endpoint.connect(peer, ALPN))
+     .await
+     .context("connect timeout")??;

- let peer_snapshot = recv.read_to_end(MAX_SNAPSHOT).await?;
+ let peer_snapshot = timeout(IO_TIMEOUT, recv.read_to_end(MAX_SNAPSHOT))
+     .await
+     .context("read peer snapshot timeout")??;
```
</details>

   


Also applies to: 110-137, 141-164

<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```

rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@experiments/android-p2p-app/core/src/net.rs` around lines 88 - 90, The async
network methods (online, connect, accept and any stream read/write calls)
currently await indefinitely; wrap each awaited network operation with a
tokio::time::timeout and return a Result so callers can handle timeouts instead
of hanging: e.g., change the signature of online/connect/accept to return
Result<_, Error>, use tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(N),
self.endpoint.online()) (or similar per call) and map timeout::error() into a
descriptive timeout error, apply the same timeout wrapper around

the error) so slow/non‑responsive peers do not block forever.

### coderabbitai[bot] on `experiments/android-p2p-app/core/src/protocol.rs` :L110 — 2026-06-13T13:51:52Z

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Bound invite payload size before decoding.**

`Invite::decode` accepts arbitrary-length input from `JoinGroup { invite }`. A large base64 body can force large allocations during decode/JSON parse and stall or OOM the process.

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
 const INVITE_PREFIX: &str = "croftcgrp1:";
+const MAX_INVITE_BODY_LEN: usize = 8 * 1024; // tune as needed

 pub fn decode(s: &str) -> Result<Self> {
     let body = s
         .strip_prefix(INVITE_PREFIX)
         .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("not a group invite (bad prefix)"))?;
+    if body.len() > MAX_INVITE_BODY_LEN {
+        return Err(anyhow!("invite too large"));
+    }
     let json = BASE64URL_NOPAD
         .decode(body.as_bytes())
         .context("base64-decode invite")?;
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@experiments/android-p2p-app/core/src/protocol.rs` around lines 91 - 99,
Invite::decode accepts unbounded base64 input which can cause large allocations;
add size checks to bound both the incoming base64 string and the decoded JSON
bytes before parsing: after obtaining body via strip_prefix(INVITE_PREFIX),
reject if body.len() exceeds a new constant MAX_INVITE_B64_LEN (e.g. 4KB–16KB as
appropriate), then after BASE64URL_NOPAD.decode(...) check that json.len() <=
MAX_INVITE_JSON_LEN (or reuse a single MAX_INVITE_BYTES) and return an error if
exceeded; keep these checks in the same function (Invite::decode) and use clear
error contexts (e.g., "invite too large (base64)" / "invite too large
(decoded)") before calling serde_json::from_slice.

### coderabbitai[bot] on `experiments/android-p2p-app/android/app/src/main/java/com/croftc/p2pexp/MainActivity.kt` :L53 — 2026-06-13T14:26:04Z

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Guard `close()` so `super.onDestroy()` always runs.**

If `client.close()` throws (FFI boundary can fail), `super.onDestroy()` is skipped, leaving Android lifecycle cleanup incomplete.





<details>
<summary>🔧 Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
     override fun onDestroy() {
         // GroupClient owns native state (the iroh node + Automerge doc). Release it
         // so a recreated Activity doesn't leak the previous session's resources.
-        client.close()
+        runCatching { client.close() }
         super.onDestroy()
     }
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
    override fun onDestroy() {
        // GroupClient owns native state (the iroh node + Automerge doc). Release it
        // so a recreated Activity doesn't leak the previous session's resources.
        runCatching { client.close() }
        super.onDestroy()
    }
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

around lines 48 - 53, The onDestroy implementation calls client.close() directly
which can throw and prevent super.onDestroy() from running; wrap the
client.close() call in a try/catch (catch Throwable) inside the onDestroy method
(referencing onDestroy, client.close(), and super.onDestroy()) so any exception
